Central Living at Craig (model), Charlotte, NC 28205
listings.description: YOUR BENCHMARK DEAL: David Weekley model with leaseback — builder pays rent + HOA until closing; washer/dryer/blinds negotiated in lieu of commission reduction (per Jon Wian email thread). Use these exact terms as the yardstick for every deal below.

listings.address: Cherry Grove Townhomes — Juniper model, Kannapolis, NC 28081
listings.description: Home-of-the-Month from your inbox: $269,990 with $5K incentive (NVR Mortgage). Best raw price-per-foot on the board ($137/sqft) in the metro's best growth-to-price submarket. Leaseback unlikely (NVR runs asset-light) — this is a price play, not a leaseback play. Rep: Vineta Pritchard.

listings.address: Sterling Pointe (model), 3414 Beatties Ford Rd, Charlotte, NC 28216
listings.description: CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): Lennar community approaching closeout in the West Charlotte path-of-progress corridor. Lennar's model program commonly includes 9–18 month leaseback and furniture conveyance at closeout. Ask: 'When does the Sterling Pointe model release, and is it sold furnished with leaseback?'

CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): Taylor Morrison runs a marketed model-home sale-leaseback program (typically 12–24 months, builder maintains the home, ~5-7% leaseback yield). Bryton is their north-corridor master-plan with townhome product near your ceiling. Ask for the model home investment program sheet.

listings.address: Mallard Pointe (model), 3011 Johnston Oehler Rd, Charlotte, NC 28269
listings.description: CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): DRH sells models at community closeout, sometimes with furnishings. University submarket rents well (UNCC + light rail). Leaseback only if the sales office still needs the space — ask about the closeout timeline.

listings.address: University Townes (model), 4912 Sunset Rd, Charlotte, NC 28269
listings.description: CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): affordable-segment model at closeout with a small cut already on the board. Furniture occasionally negotiable; leaseback unusual for this builder. A price-first fallback if the leaseback hunts stall.

CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): True Homes is the big local builder (Monroe HQ) — local decision-makers say yes/no fast. Model leasebacks negotiable at grand openings of successor communities; Union County's low taxes (0.77%) flatter the cash flow.

CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): Meritage does model-sale leasebacks near closeout case-by-case; energy-spec homes rent at a premium and Cabarrus is the metro's best growth-to-price county. Under budget with furniture.

listings.address: Sugar Creek Townes (model), Indian Land, SC 29707
listings.description: CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): Mattamy does Carolinas model leasebacks — BUT this one carries the SC trap: non-owner-occupied SC property is assessed at a 6% ratio, roughly tripling property tax vs an NC equivalent during the leaseback. Run the numbers tab before pursuing.

listings.address: Robinwood Commons (model), 4412 Robinwood Rd, Gastonia, NC 28054
listings.description: CURATED LEAD (verify by phone): cheapest model-candidate on the board. Usually sold unfurnished without leaseback — pure entry-price play in the I-85 momentum corridor.

submarkets.label: Uptown / South End
submarkets.note: Premium core; supply-heavy condo/TH pipeline caps near-term upside.

submarkets.label: Plaza Midwood / NoDa
submarkets.note: Strong rental demand from young professionals; your Central Living at Craig deal sits here.

submarkets.label: West Charlotte / Wesley Heights
submarkets.note: Path-of-progress: Gold Line + River District spillover. Best appreciation bet inside the city.

submarkets.label: East Charlotte
submarkets.note: Cheapest Mecklenburg entry with real rent support; watch specific pockets.

submarkets.label: University / North Charlotte
submarkets.note: UNCC + light rail anchor rents; heavy builder activity keeps prices honest.

submarkets.label: Steele Creek / Airport South
submarkets.note: Jobs corridor (airport, distribution); steady tenant pool.

submarkets.label: Ballantyne / South Charlotte
submarkets.note: Schools premium priced in; thinner cash flow.

submarkets.label: Mooresville
submarkets.note: Lower Iredell taxes help cash flow.

submarkets.label: Concord / Kannapolis
submarkets.note: Best affordability-to-growth ratio in the metro. Sub-$300K new TH still exists here.

submarkets.label: Gastonia / Belmont
submarkets.note: Cheapest entry in the metro; I-85 corridor momentum, higher Gaston taxes.

submarkets.label: Indian Land, SC
submarkets.note: CAUTION for leaseback/investor: SC assesses non-owner-occupied at 6% ratio — property tax roughly triples vs owner-occupied.

submarkets.label: Fort Mill / Tega Cay, SC
submarkets.note: Top schools; same SC 6% investor-assessment caution as Indian Land.

submarkets.label: Waxhaw / Monroe
submarkets.note: Union Co. low taxes; Monroe is the value end, Waxhaw the premium end.

leaseback_programs.terms: Sells models with leaseback; builder pays rent + HOA until closing/community build-out. Your Central Living at Craig deal is exactly this program — washer/dryer/blinds were negotiable in lieu of commission.
leaseback_programs.tip: You are already a repeat buyer; ask Jon which Charlotte models are next to be released.

leaseback_programs.terms: Marketed model-home sale-leaseback program to investors; typical 12–24 mo leaseback at ~5-7% yield on price, builder maintains the home.
leaseback_programs.tip: Ask for the "model home investment program" sheet — it is a standing program, not a favor.

leaseback_programs.terms: Sells models with leaseback through community completion (commonly 9–18 mo); furniture packages frequently convey on closeout models.
leaseback_programs.tip: Closeout communities (last phase) are where furnished models get priced to move.

leaseback_programs.terms: Model sales with leaseback happen near community closeout; energy-spec homes rent well.
leaseback_programs.tip: Ask about Concord/Cabarrus communities to stay under $350K.

leaseback_programs.terms: Largest builder = most model inventory. Models typically sold at community closeout, occasionally furnished; leaseback when the sales office still needs the space.
leaseback_programs.tip: Volume means deals: ask each community "when does this model release, and will you lease it back?"

leaseback_programs.terms: Does model leasebacks in Carolinas communities, mostly at grand-opening phases of the next community.
leaseback_programs.tip: Indian Land presence — remember the SC 6% investor tax before chasing these.

leaseback_programs.terms: Large local builder (Monroe/Gastonia/Concord heavy); sells models at closeout, leaseback negotiable at grand openings.
leaseback_programs.tip: Local decision-makers = faster yes/no than nationals.

leaseback_programs.terms: NVR runs asset-light and rarely owns models long; closeout model sales happen (often unfurnished), leaseback uncommon.
leaseback_programs.tip: Their value is price, not leaseback — e.g. Cherry Grove Juniper at $269,990.

leaseback_programs.terms: Affordable-segment models sold at closeout; furniture occasionally negotiable, leaseback unusual.
leaseback_programs.tip: Best raw price-per-foot if leaseback is optional for you.
Gastonia communities are the value pocket.

leaseback_programs.terms: Periodic model leasebacks, usually in larger master-planned communities.
leaseback_programs.tip: Most Pulte TH product runs above $350K — watch for incentives.

leaseback_programs.terms: Regular model leasebacks with designer furnishings — but Charlotte TH product starts well above $350K.
leaseback_programs.tip: Out of budget for this hunt; listed for completeness.

assumptions.disclaimer: Curated estimates for new-construction townhomes, mid-2026. Not an appraisal or lending quote — verify rent comps and tax bills per address before writing an offer.
